text: The current OpenCut rewrite is not a usable editor integration target; the archived classic repository is the only current source-level editor reference and must be treated as selective source material, not an SDK.
counter_search: Checked current rewrite routes and roadmap for a complete editor implementation; none was present, while the official README directs users to classic.

text: shadcn/ui should be added as owned source code, and its canonical Sidebar supplies the responsive and accessible primitives needed for the VideoBox app shell.
counter_search: Checked whether shadcn/ui is intended as a conventional runtime component package; official material describes source distribution and CLI-generated owned code instead.

text: shadcn-admin is suitable as a selective shell source but not as a whole-project template or runtime dependency.
counter_search: Compared the deployed shell with repository guidance and dependency boundaries; the README explicitly says the project is not a starter template.

text: Supabase Studio is valuable for project and settings information architecture, but direct source porting would import a large product-specific dependency graph and should remain reference-only for this migration.
counter_search: Inspected whether the relevant layouts were standalone components; they rely on Studio workspace packages, routing, data hooks, feature gates, and telemetry.

text: Opencast Editor offers useful waveform, cut, subtitle cue, and keyboard interaction patterns, while its Redux, MUI, player, API client, and browser waveform pipeline should not be imported wholesale into VideoBox.
counter_search: Inspected whether transcript and timeline behaviors were isolated packages; the visible workflow exists, but implementation remains coupled to Opencast state and endpoints.

text: Pretendard can be self-hosted for consistent Korean typography, provided the SIL Open Font License notice is distributed and no runtime CDN is used.
counter_search: Checked the official license and package metadata for a conflicting proprietary or network-only distribution requirement; none was found.
